wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the project overview section focused on the server-authoritative 3D multiplayer FPS game built with Unity and Unity Gaming Services. Explain the project's purpose as a graduation thesis demonstrating scalable multiplayer architecture with AI bot integration, its core technologies (Unity, Netcode for GameObjects, Unity Relay, Unity Lobby), and key architectural decisions. Document the hybrid FSM-BT AI system, zone-based spatial reasoning, and hierarchical pathfinding approach. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners understanding multiplayer game architecture and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase including terms like 'server-authoritative', 'client-host topology', 'behavior trees', and 'tactical AI'. Provide practical examples demonstrating the integration of Unity services and the overall game flow from authentication to gameplay.

wiki_catalogs.name: Layered Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the layered architecture system. Explain the five-layer design pattern including Presentation Layer (Unity UI), Networking Layer (Unity Relay + Lobby + NGO), Game Logic Layer (Server-Authoritative Management), AI Layer (Hybrid FSM-BT + Zone-Based Spatial AI), and Data Layer (ScriptableObjects + NetworkVariables). Document the clear separation of concerns, data flow between layers, and how each layer interacts with adjacent layers. Include architectural diagrams showing layer relationships and explain the rationale behind this layered approach for scalability and maintainability. Address how the server-authoritative design is implemented across layers and the role of each layer in the overall system architecture.

wiki_overview.content:
# FPS Game 20260423 - Comprehensive Project Overview ## Project Introduction This project is a **graduation thesis** presenting the development of a **server-authoritative 3D multiplayer FPS game** built using Unity and Unity Gaming Services. The system implements a sophisticated hybrid architecture combining **Netcode for GameObjects (NGO)** for real-time synchronization, **Unity Relay** for serverless connectivity, and **Unity Lobby** for matchmaking and session management. ### Core Goals and Objectives - **Multiplayer Architecture**: Demonstrate scalable server-authoritative multiplayer FPS design - **AI Integration**: Implement AI-controlled bots with network synchronization across all clients - **Hybrid AI System**: Combine Finite State Machines with Behavior Trees for intelligent bot decision-making - **Spatial Reasoning**: Develop zone-based tactical AI with hierarchical pathfinding systems ### Target Audience - Unity developers seeking to understand multiplayer game architecture - Students studying AI implementation in games - Game designers interested in tactical AI systems - Developers exploring Unity Gaming Services integration ## Technical Architecture The project follows a **layered architecture** with five distinct layers, providing clear separation of concerns and maintainable code organization: ```mermaid graph TB subgraph "Presentation Layer" UI[Unity UI System
Sign In, Lobby, HUD, Scoreboard] end subgraph "Networking Layer" Relay[Unity Relay Server] Lobby[Unity Lobby Service] NGO[Netcode for GameObjects] end subgraph "Game Logic Layer" GameManager[Server-Authoritative Game Session] MatchFlow[Match Lifecycle Management] Sync[Network Synchronization] end subgraph "AI Layer" FSM[FSM Bot Controller] BT[Behavior Designer Trees] Perception[Perception Sensors] ZoneMgr[Zone Management System] end subgraph "Data Layer" Scriptables[ScriptableObjects] NetworkVars[NetworkVariables] PlayerData[Player Statistics] end UI --> Relay Relay --> NGO NGO --> GameManager GameManager --> FSM FSM --> BT BT --> ZoneMgr ZoneMgr --> Scriptables GameManager --> NetworkVars ``` ### Key Design Patterns The architecture employs several fundamental design patterns: - **Singleton Pattern**: Used for global managers like `InGameManager` - **Observer Pattern**: Implemented through event systems for AI perception and networking - **State Machine Pattern**: FSM for bot behavior control - **Component Pattern**: Unity's component-based architecture for modular gameplay systems ## Key Implementation ### Main Entry Points **Lobby Management System** - [`Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Lobby Script/Lobby/Scripts/LobbyManager.cs`](file://Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Lobby%20Script/Lobby/Scripts/LobbyManager.cs) - [`Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Lobby Script/Lobby/Scripts/Relay.cs`](file://Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Lobby%20Script/Lobby/Scripts/Relay.cs) **Core Gameplay Systems** - [`Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/System/InGameManager.cs`](file://Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/System/InGameManager.cs) - [`Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Player/PlayerController.cs`](file://Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Player/PlayerController.cs) - [`Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Player/PlayerShoot.cs`](file://Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Player/PlayerShoot.cs) ### AI Bot System Architecture The AI system implements a **hybrid FSM-Behavior Tree** architecture: ```mermaid stateDiagram-v2 [*] --> Idle [*] --> Patrol [*] --> Combat Idle --> Patrol: Timeout (5-10s) Patrol --> Combat: Player Detected Combat --> Patrol: Player Dead/Lost Patrol --> Patrol: Zone Fully Scanned Patrol --> Idle: Random Timeout ``` **BotController States**: 1. **Idle State**: Bot stands still, performs random look-around behavior 2. **Patrol State**: Navigates through zones using Dijkstra pathfinding 3. **Combat State**: Engages detected players with tactical movement **Behavior Designer Integration**: - Separate behavior trees for each state (Idle, Patrol, Combat) - BlackboardLinker bridges C# data with Behavior Designer SharedVariables - AIInputFeeder converts behavior outputs to player inputs ### Networking Architecture **Server-Authoritative Design**: ```mermaid sequenceDiagram participant Client participant Server participant NGO participant BotController Client->>+Server: Player Input Server->>+NGO: Validate & Process NGO->>+BotController: Network Sync BotController->>+Server: Bot Decisions Server->>+Client: State Updates Client->>+Client: Render Updates ``` **Network Synchronization Flow**: 1. Client sends input to server via NGO 2. Server validates and processes actions 3. Server updates authoritative game state 4. NGO automatically syncs state to all clients 5. Clients render updated state ### Zone-Based Spatial Reasoning **Zone System Architecture**: ```mermaid classDiagram class Zone { +ZoneID zoneID +List~InfoPoint~ infoPoints +List~TacticalPoint~ tacticalPoints +List~PortalPoint~ portals +float zoneWeight } class ZoneManager { +List~Zone~ allZones +List~PortalPoint~ allPortals +Dictionary~ZoneID,Zone~ zoneCache +CalculatePath() +FindBestZone() } class BotTactics { +InitializeZoneScanning() +CalculateCurrentVisiblePoint() +PredictMostSuspiciousZone() } ZoneManager --> Zone ZoneManager --> PortalPoint BotTactics --> Zone BotTactics --> InfoPoint ``` **Pathfinding Implementation**: - **Strategic Level**: Dijkstra's algorithm on zone graph - **Local Level**: Unity NavMesh for precise navigation - **Hierarchical Approach**: Combines both methods for optimal performance ### Configuration Management **Project Settings**: - [`ProjectSettings/ProjectSettings.asset`](file://ProjectSettings/ProjectSettings.asset) - [`ProjectSettings/PackageManagerSettings.asset`](file://ProjectSettings/PackageManagerSettings.asset) - [`ProjectSettings/NetcodeForGameObjects.asset`](file://ProjectSettings/NetcodeForGameObjects.asset) **Configuration Patterns**: - ScriptableObjects for editor-time data baking - NetworkVariables for automatic synchronization - Event-driven architecture for decoupled systems ## Key Features ### Multiplayer System **Lobby Management**: - Real-time lobby creation and joining - Player authentication via Unity Authentication - Dynamic bot count adjustment - Heartbeat and polling mechanisms **Match Flow**: ```mermaid flowchart TD SignIn[Sign In] --> LobbyList[Lobby List] LobbyList --> LobbyRoom[Lobby Room] LobbyRoom --> GameStart[Game Start] GameStart --> MatchPlay[Match Play] MatchPlay --> MatchEnd[Match End] MatchEnd --> LobbyList subgraph "Server Operations" Relay[Unity Relay] LobbySvc[Unity Lobby Service] Auth[Unity Authentication] end subgraph "Client Operations" ClientInput[Player Input] NetworkSync[Network Sync] Render[Visual Feedback] end LobbyRoom --> Relay GameStart --> Relay ClientInput --> NetworkSync NetworkSync --> Render ``` ### AI Bot Capabilities **Perception System**: - Sphere casting for player detection - Raycasting for line-of-sight validation - Last-known position tracking - Event-driven state transitions **Tactical AI**: - Zone scanning with InfoPoint prioritization - Suspicious zone prediction algorithms - Tactical positioning using TacticalPoints - Hierarchical pathfinding implementation ### Weapon System **Server-Authoritative Damage**: - Client-side prediction with server validation - Hit registration based on body parts - Damage calculation per weapon type - Network RPC for damage processing **Weapon Types Supported**: - Rifle: Balanced damage and accuracy - Sniper: High damage, long range - Pistol: Close combat effectiveness - Melee: Tactical close-range combat ### Performance Considerations **Optimization Strategies**: - Network compression for reduced bandwidth - Client-side prediction for responsive controls - Server-authoritative validation for cheat prevention - Hierarchical AI decision-making for CPU efficiency **Scalability Features**: - Variable bot count support - Configurable match settings - Modular system architecture - Extensible behavior tree system ## Conclusion and Recommendations ### Architectural Strengths 1. **Robust Multiplayer Foundation**: Excellent implementation of server-authoritative architecture 2. **Sophisticated AI System**: Innovative hybrid FSM-BT approach with spatial reasoning 3. **Clear Separation of Concerns**: Well-organized layered architecture 4. **Extensible Design**: Modular components easily adaptable for future features ### Potential Improvements 1. **UI/UX Enhancement**: Current interface needs modernization and polish 2. **Animation System**: Character animations require refinement for professional feel 3. **Network Optimization**: Address latency issues between host and clients 4. **AI Behavior Polishing**: Improve bot movement realism and tactical complexity ### Future Development Roadmap **Short-term (Post-Thesis)**: - UI/UX overhaul with modern design patterns - Enhanced character animations and effects - Performance profiling and optimization **Medium-term**: - Advanced combat behaviors (cover seeking, flanking) - Multiple game modes (Team Deathmatch, Bomb Defusal) - Enhanced AI perception systems **Long-term Vision**: - Cross-platform deployment (PC, consoles, mobile) - Advanced matchmaking and ranking systems - Persistent world elements and progression --- **Sources:** - [README.md](README.md) - [WIKI.md](WIKI.md) - [LobbyManager.cs](Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Lobby%20Script/Lobby/Scripts/LobbyManager.cs) - [InGameManager.cs](Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/System/InGameManager.cs) - [BotController.cs](Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Bot/BotController.cs) - [PlayerController.cs](Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Player/PlayerController.cs) - [PlayerShoot.cs](Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/Player/PlayerShoot.cs) - [ZoneController.cs](Assets/FPS-Game/Scripts/System/ZoneController.cs) - [ProjectSettings.asset](ProjectSettings/ProjectSettings.asset) - [PackageManagerSettings.asset](ProjectSettings/PackageManagerSettings.asset) - [NetcodeForGameObjects.asset](ProjectSettings/NetcodeForGameObjects.asset)
